component.name: clpeak
component.summary: Measure peak compute, bandwidth and latency of GPU and CPU devices

description.p: clpeak is a synthetic micro-benchmark that measures the peak achievable performance of GPU and CPU compute devices. It exercises tight vector / MAD / MMA loops and vendor-SDK GEMM libraries to expose what the hardware is actually capable of, from raw ALU peaks to near-vendor-advertised matrix throughput.
This Flatpak enables the backends that work inside the sandbox:

ul.li: Vulkan — compute, bandwidth, cooperative-matrix (tensor) and latency
OpenCL — when the host exposes an ICD the sandbox can see
Native CPU — SIMD compute, cache/DRAM bandwidth and memory latency

description.p: Benchmarks include global/local/image memory bandwidth, single/half/double precision compute, integer throughput, tensor / cooperative-matrix operations, and kernel launch latency. Results can be written as plain text, XML, JSON or CSV, and compared against a saved baseline.

The CUDA, ROCm, oneAPI and Metal backends are not available in this Flatpak because they require vendor toolkits or proprietary drivers that cannot be shipped through Flathub; use the upstream binary releases for those.
